title: install.ps1: write config.json from the binary's canonical output
action: Replace install.ps1's hand-written config hashtable literal (around line 132-140, ConvertTo-Json, missing agent_presets per D1) with a call into the just-installed binary: inside the existing `if (-not (Test-Path $ConfigFile))` guard, capture the binary's stdout as a string (e.g. `$defaultConfig = & $BinPath --internal-print-default-config; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0 -or -not $defaultConfig) { Die '...' }`) and write it with `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($ConfigFile, $defaultConfig, (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false)))` -- mirroring the SAME no-BOM write pattern the file already uses for this exact config write today (the current ConvertTo-Json block ends with `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($ConfigFile, ($config | ConvertTo-Json), (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false)))`). Do NOT use `Out-File -Encoding utf8` or any cmdlet that defaults to writing a UTF-8 BOM: on Windows PowerShell 5.1 that BOM is real, serde_json does not skip it, and a BOM'd config.json fails to parse at the binary's first start -- this is exactly the failure mode WriteAllText+UTF8Encoding($false) already exists in this file to avoid. Keep the same idempotent only-if-missing guard, same $ConfigFile path, same 'Wrote default config'/'Existing config left untouched' messaging. Per D6, this closes the gap where fresh Windows installs never got agent_presets. Note: this environment has no pwsh available to syntax-check the script directly -- release.yml's existing 'Real install.ps1 -> Scheduled Task...' smoke job is the real end-to-end proof, run in CI against a published release, not locally.

truths: A fresh install.ps1 run on a $HOME with no existing config.json (once actually run on Windows/CI) produces a config.json containing agent_presets
Re-running install.ps1 when config.json already exists leaves it untouched (idempotent guard preserved)
config.json is written without a UTF-8 BOM

artifacts.substantive: config.json is written by capturing herdr-go --internal-print-default-config output via WriteAllText+UTF8Encoding($false); no hand-written hashtable/ConvertTo-Json literal remains; no Out-File/BOM-producing write

prohibitions: No change to $ConfigFile's path or the surrounding Scheduled Task registration logic
No Out-File or other BOM-producing write for $ConfigFile -- must use [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText with UTF8Encoding($false), matching the file's existing no-BOM pattern

verify: ! grep -q "bind_addr        = '0.0.0.0:8787'" install.ps1 && grep -q -- '--internal-print-default-config' install.ps1 && ! grep -q 'Out-File' install.ps1 && grep -q 'UTF8Encoding(\$false)' install.ps1 && cargo run --quiet --bin herdr-go -- --internal-print-default-config | grep -q agent_presets

trace.outcome: install.ps1's config-write block now captures 'herdr-go --internal-print-default-config' stdout and writes it verbatim via WriteAllText+UTF8Encoding($false), replacing the hand-written ConvertTo-Json hashtable literal that was missing agent_presets; no Out-File/BOM-producing write introduced; idempotent only-if-missing guard and $ConfigFile path unchanged.
